gift: Not a present! Means poison — and also "married" (blive gift = get married). A present is "en gave".
gave: This IS the word for a present/gift. Not the past tense of English "give".
slut: Means "end" or "finished" (slut på filmen = end of the movie). Nothing rude.
fart: Means speed (fuld fart = full speed). Nothing rude. You'll see it on road signs.
mad: Means food, not angry or crazy.
bad: Means bath or shower, not bad. "Dårlig" means bad.
fire: Means four (4), not fire. Fire is "ild" or "brand".
barn: Means child, not a farm building. Plural is børn.
dog: Means "however / though", not a dog. A dog is "en hund".
sky: Means cloud (and also gravy, and "shy"). The sky is "himlen".
men: Means "but", not men (the people). Men are "mænd".
kind: Means cheek, not kind. Kind is "sød" or "venlig".
time: Means hour (or a lesson), not time. Time is "tid". Pronounced "tee-meh".
sort: Means black, not sort/kind. A kind is "en slags".
sand: As an adjective means "true"; as a noun it does mean sand.
rat: Means steering wheel, not a rat. A rat is "en rotte".
fag: Means school subject or trade/profession. Completely neutral word in Danish.
student: Means someone who finished gymnasium (high school), not any student. A university student is "en studerende".
aktuel: Means current/topical, not actual. Actual is "faktisk".
eventuel: Means possible / "if any", not eventual. Eventually is "til sidst".
art: Means species or kind, not art. Art is "kunst".
blanket: Means a form to fill in, not a blanket. A blanket is "et tæppe".
brik: Means a game piece, not a brick. A brick is "en mursten".
chef: Means boss/manager, not a cook. A chef is "en kok".
fabrik: Means factory, not fabric. Fabric is "stof".
fyr: Means a guy (or a lighthouse, or a pine tree), not fire.
skum: Means foam (like on a beer), not scum.
branche: Means industry / line of business, not a tree branch (that's "en gren").
hjemmeside: Means a whole website, not just the home page.
mail: Means e-mail specifically. Physical mail is "post".
spotte: Means to mock someone, not to spot. To spot is "at få øje på".
grine: Means to laugh, not to grin. To smile is "at smile".
rente: Means (bank) interest, not rent. Rent is "husleje".
kommune: Means municipality (your local government), not a commune.
pensel: Means paintbrush, not pencil. A pencil is "en blyant".
pig: Means a spike, not a pig. A pig is "en gris".
novelle: Means a short story, not a novel. A novel is "en roman".
klit: Means a sand dune. Common in beach place names — nothing rude.
kasserolle: Means saucepan, not a casserole dish.
spejder: Means a scout (boy/girl scout), not a spider. A spider is "en edderkop".
lever: Means liver (and "lives", from at leve), not a lever.
